description:

title: US withdraw more than 4,000 troops from Europe
description: The US Department of War has announced the withdrawal of a Brigade Combat Team, comprising at least 4,000 troops, from Europe.

title: Kane to miss Bayern game against Gladbach with calf knock
description:
BERLIN: Harry Kane will miss Bayern Munich’s Bundesliga match against Borussia Moenchengladbach with a calf injury, coach Vincent Kompany said on Thursday.
	The England striker, who has been an ever-present for Bayern this season, should be fit in time for the Champions League last-16 first leg at Atalanta next Tuesday.
	“Harry Kane is out. He got a knock on his calf and hasn’t recovered yet,” Kompany said at a pre-match press conference ahead of

title: PSG prepare for Chelsea clash with Monaco rematch
description:
PARIS: Paris Saint-Germain warm up for their Champions League last-16 showdown with Chelsea by once again taking on Monaco on Friday, as coach Luis Enrique looked forward to “the best part of the season” starting.
	PSG have not managed to rediscover the exhilarating form that led them to Champions League glory for the first time in their history last season, but they come into March still on course to retain their French and European titles.

title: Swedish intelligence warns of increased Middle East war fallout
description:
STOCKHOLM: A top Swedish intelligence official on Thursday warned of increased threats against American and Israeli interests, as well as Iranian dissidents, in Sweden because of the war in the Middle East.
	The Scandinavian country’s security, which had already deteriorated after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, has worsened since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran on Saturday unleashed a new war, according to Fredrik Hallstrom, head of operations for

title: El-Sisi says Egypt in ‘state of near-emergency’ as war threatens economy
description:
CAIRO: Egyptian President Abdel Fattah El-Sisi said Thursday his country was in an economic “state of near-emergency” as a result of the Middle East war, warning of runaway inflation.
	The Arab world’s most populous nation has not been physically impacted by the US and Israeli war with Iran, which has seen strikes on Egypt’s wealthy Gulf allies and paralyzed trade through the vital Strait of Hormuz.

title: How Israel’s ‘buffer zone’ plan could redraw the map of southern Lebanon
description:
BEIRUT: Lebanon faces what officials described as “one of the most dangerous moments in recent memory” after Hezbollah launched attacks on Israeli forces from areas both south and north of the Litani River, triggering a renewed Israeli air and ground campaign.

	Announcing its ground incursion into southern Lebanon, Israel said it aims to establish what it called a permanent “security zone” along the border.

title: Europol warns Iran crisis raises threat of terror, extremism and cyberattacks
description:
MADRID: The Middle East conflict will have “immediate repercussions” for European Union security with an increased threat of terrorism, serious and organized crime as well as violent extremism and cyberattacks, European police body Europol told Spanish news agency EFE on Thursday.
	Europol spokesman Jan Op Gen Oorth said he expected to see more cyberattacks against European infrastructure and an increase in online fraud using increasingly sophist

title: Israel decided to kill Khamenei in November, defense minister says
description:
JERUSALEM: Israel took the decision to kill Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in November and was planning to carry out the operation around six months later, Defense Minister Israel Katz said on Thursday.
	Khamenei was killed in the first hours of the US-Israeli air campaign that began on Saturday in the first assassination of a country’s top ruler by an airstrike.

title: Macron calls on Israel, Iran and Hezbollah to prevent Lebanon being embroiled in conflict
description:
LONDON: Emmanuel Macron on Thursday called on Israel and Iran not to embroil Lebanon in the conflict sweeping the Middle East.

	The French president made his plea as panic swept through Beirut after Israel ordered residents to evacuate the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital and three villages in Lebanon’s eastern Bekaa region.

	Macron said he had drawn up a plan to end hostilities between Israel and the Iran-backed Lebanese militia Hezbol

title: Jordan’s king condemns Iranian attacks in region during talks with Syrian president
description:
LONDON: King Abdullah of Jordan and President Ahmad Al-Sharaa of Syria discussed on Thursday the dangerous developments in the region this week following the US and Israeli attacks against Iran that began on Saturday.

	During their telephone conversation, both leaders emphasized the need for dialogue to prevent further escalation of the regional crisis.

title: From Serena to Coco: How to Coach a Tennis Superstar
description:
Roland Garros begins this weekend, as the world’s best players arrive in Paris for the 130th edition of the French Open.
 
In the men’s draw, world number one Jannik Sinner returns after last year’s epic final defeat to Carlos Alcaraz. In the women’s competition, Aryna Sabalenka arrives as the top-ranked player, while attention will also focus on Iga Swiatek — the Queen of Clay — and defending champion Coco Gauff.

title: Trump intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard resigns
description: Tulsi Gabbard resigned Friday as President Donald Trump’s director of national intelligence, saying she is stepping down to support her husband after his diagnosis with a rare bone cancer. Her departure marks the fourth Cabinet-level exit of Trump’s second term and comes amid growing tensions over the administration’s Iran policy after Gabbard’s public testimony at times diverged from the president’s claims.

title: French Gaza flotilla activists return to Paris, describe 'violent' detention by Israel
description: The first group of French activists from the Global Sumud aid flotilla returned to Paris on Friday after being detained and then deported by Israel amid international outcry. They described what they said was a violent, humiliating ordeal – including sexual abuse – after they were detained by Israeli forces during the group's third attempt to break the blockade of the beleaguered Gaza Strip.

title: Trump disappointed in NATO: Plan B?
description:
As NATO foreign ministers gather in southern Sweden, fresh questions are growing over President Trump’s commitment to the alliance.
 
The message from Washington today came via Secretary of State Marco Rubio, amid continuing uncertainty over Article 5 — NATO’s collective defence guarantee. In recent months, President Trump has raised doubts over US support for allies, alongside reports of possible troop withdrawals from Europe and tensions over G

title: Haiti's children trapped by gangs face an uncertain future despite new UN security force
description: Haiti’s powerful gangs nearly tripled their recruitment of children in 2025. This year, a new UN-backed security force has begun deploying – but for the thousands of children already caught up in armed groups, the path back to a stable future remains uncertain.

title: Monica Bellucci returns to Cannes for dark thriller 'The Birthday Party'
description: Monica Bellucci brings classic Cannes glamour back to the Croisette tonight as she premieres 'The Birthday Party', the latest Competition film from acclaimed French director Léa Mysius. Bellucci plays a mysterious Italian painter living next door, alongside Hafsia Herzi and Bastien Bouil

description: First, at least 160 lives already feared lost, health workers in the DRC are racing against time to contain a deadly disease that spreads as fast as it kills. Also, a diplomatic rift with the European Union is widening over security funding in Mozambique. The Foreign Ministry has criticised Brussels for halting support for Rwandan troops deployed in Cabo Delgado. And, African animation is stepping out of the margins with bold global ambitions. As

title: Putin Xi, To Catch a Castro, Red Carpet Rebellion
description:

In Washington, the Trump administration has indicted 94-year-old former Cuban president Raúl Castro over alleged murder and conspiracy to kill US nationals, linked to the 1990s downing of two civilian aircraft — a move officials describe as long-delayed justice, but which critics see as part of a wider push on regime change.
 
In Beijing,

description: The Warsh era begins with soaring inflation, a Middle East energy shock bleeding into other parts of the economy and colleagues skeptical that rate cuts should come anytime soon.Add on top: Kevin Warsh faces more political pressure to deliver lower rates than any other Federal Reserve chair in recent memory.Why it matters: The 17th Fed chair, sworn in Friday at the White House, inherits a set of economic conditions that make it difficult to justi

title: Italy settles on Airbus tanker purchase in swing toward European equipment
description: The deal marks a shift from Boeing to Airbus for the Italian Air Force, which flies four Boeing 767-based tanker aircraft which entered service from 2011.

title: Air France flight to US diverted to Montreal due to Ebola travel restrictions
description: An Air France flight bound for Detroit was diverted to Montreal after a passenger from the Congo boarded a flight in Paris “in error” amid flight restrictions tied to the Ebola outbreak, US Customs and Border Protection said Thursday. A spokesperson for the agency says the passenger “should not have boarded” the plane Wednesday due to US entry restrictions put in place to reduce the risk of Ebola spreading.

title: Gaza flotilla: Activists allege abuse, sexual assault on return from Israel
description: Hundreds of activists from the Global Sumud Gaza flotilla, some covered in bruises and others in wheelchairs or stretchers, arrived in Turkey on Thursday after being deported from Israel. Many alleged abuse at the hands of Israeli forces, including beatings and sexual assault.

title: French Open: Players accuse Slams of ignoring concerns as tensions rise
description: The dispute between players and the Grand Slams reaches boiling point in Paris, but boycott restricted to media duties.

title: Ukrainian strike on dormitory in Russian-occupied Starobilsk kills four
description: A Ukrainian strike collapsed a five-storey college dormitory in the Russian-occupied city of Starobilsk, killing four.

title: Western nations warn Israel to end illegal settlement expansion, violence
description: Nine countries including UK, Germany and France warn settlements in occupied West Bank a breach of international law.

title: Economic confidence plummets in US amid Iran war, poll shows
description: As petrol prices rise, new survey suggests economic confidence in the US is at -45, the worst since 2022.

title: Regional Mediators Rush to Save U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire
description: Pakistan and Qatar have dispatched teams to Tehran under the looming threat of resumed war, after weeks of diplomacy failed to produce an agreement.

title: Europeans Remain Wary After Trump Promises to Send Troops to Poland
description: President Trump said he would send 5,000 troops to Poland, part of a back-and-forth that has left European leaders reeling.

title: Malaysia appoints new anti-graft chief
description:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia appointed on Saturday a former high court judge as the country’s new anti-graft czar to replace its outgoing chief who was dogged by allegations of abuse of power and illegal shareholding.
	The appointment of Abdul Halim Aman comes as Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim’s government faces increased scrutiny on promises made to tackle corruption in the Southeast Asian nation.

title: Chinese panda pair headed to US zoo
description:
BEIJING: Two giant pandas from China are headed to Atlanta on a new 10-year conservation deal.
	Pandas Ping Ping, a male, and Fu Shuang, a female, will live at Zoo Atlanta, China Wildlife Conservation Association said in a statement Friday.
	The organization signed the research agreement with the United States zoo last year, it said, noting the deal continues “the ‘panda bond’ shared by the people of both nations for more than 20 years.”

title: Despite Iran tensions, King Charles III will follow his mother’s lead in celebrating US-UK bonds
description:
LONDON: The challenge for King Charles III as he embarks on this week’s state visit to the US is, as always, to live up to his mother’s example.
	The late Queen Elizabeth II wowed Congress in 1991 with a speech that celebrated the shared democratic traditions of Britain and the United States, quoted Abraham Lincoln, Franklin D. Roosevelt and Ralph Waldo Emerson, and highlighted the deep bonds between the two nations.

title: Australia refuses repatriation help for citizens in Syria camp
description:
SYDNEY: Australia refused to help repatriate citizens related to suspected Daesh militants on Saturday, following a report that some Australian families are trying to return home from a camp in Syria.
	National broadcaster ABC said four Australian women and nine of their children and grandchildren had left the Roj camp in northeastern Syria on Friday, citing the camp director.
	Syrian forces were taking them to Damascus to help them fly back to A

title: National Library acquires world’s earliest known kosher cookbook
description:
The 1846 volume, believed to have been compiled by Lady Judith Montefiore, includes some of the earliest documented cheesecake recipes in Jewish culinary literature
The post National Library acquires world’s earliest known kosher cookbook appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Iran moves to broaden its control over Hormuz, infuriating its Gulf neighbors and the US
description:
Tehran publishes map asserting its control over area that extends into coastal waters of Oman and UAE; Rubio denounces move, five Gulf states protest it, Oman reportedly discussing it with Iran
The post Iran moves to broaden its control over Hormuz, infuriating its Gulf neighbors and the US appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: European powers urge Israel to end W. Bank settlement expansion, warn of settler violence
description:
Italy, France, Britain and Germany say Jerusalem is 'undermining stability' and harming prospects for a two-state solution
The post European powers urge Israel to end W. Bank settlement expansion, warn of settler violence appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Saudi outlet publishes not-yet-approved ‘final draft’ of US-Iran deal, claims it could be announced within hours
description:
Reported text doesn't mention nuclear program or other central US demands * Key mediators said heading to Tehran * Lebanon says 10 killed in Israeli strikes on south
The post Saudi outlet publishes not-yet-approved final draft of US-Iran deal, claims it could be announced within hours appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Pakistani field marshal heads to Tehran to try to seal U.S.-Iran deal
description: Pakistan's top military commander, Field Marshal Asim Munir, is traveling to Tehran on Friday in an effort to reach a deal under which the U.S. and Iran would agree to end the war and launch negotiations for a broader agreement, a Pakistani security source told Axios.Why it matters: Munir has been the lead mediator between the U.S. and Iran since the war began. title: Taiwan says it has not been told by US of arms sales delays
description: U.S. President Donald Trump said following a summit with China’s Xi Jinping in Beijing last week that he was undecided on whether to approve it.
